anchor: HZO FeFET eNVM (HZO_5) vs SRAM, 28 nm
branch: side-branch off the silicon story: the memory-technology choice in the embodied-vs-operational trade-off (CF = OCF + (T/LT)*ECF)

panelA_per_cm2.units: gCO2e/cm2 (grams; COFFEE get_cpa() is grams despite some debug prints saying kg)

note: Density inversion: FeFET costs +9.0% carbon/cm2 (committed fefet_ald.json area-efficiency 0.8; ~+10.9% at the paper's ~0.96) but ~4.3x LOWER carbon per MB because the FeFET cell is ~5x denser (30 vs 146 F2). Net memory-tech choice is a life-cycle trade-off: a one-time fab cost amortised over operation via leakage/energy savings, and co-optimised with write-endurance (thicker HZO -> higher CPA but higher endurance/lifetime).
